response.justification: Candidate brings rare geospatial domain expertise and strong software fundamentals from multiple senior roles, but faces a significant gap in modern C++ experience (last substantial work was 2014-2015) and limited proven background in high-performance distributed systems engineering. The recent five years focus on web/product engineering rather than systems-level work. Worth exploring if candidate has kept current with C++ and is genuinely motivated by infrastructure engineering.
strengths: Rare geospatial/navigation domain expertise through GISCollective and Open Green Map projects—directly applicable to routing and mapping systems
Demonstrated senior-level engineering fundamentals with emphasis on testing, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship across diverse technical contexts
Strong learning ability and cross-domain experience, having successfully built systems across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded platforms
concerns: Last significant C++ work was at National Instruments in 2014-2015; recent five years focused on TypeScript/Node.js web engineering rather than systems programming
No demonstrated experience with high-performance distributed systems at scale; Dunnhumby's low-latency work is the closest parallel but used DLang, not C++
No Rust experience mentioned despite it being listed as acceptable alternative; would require ramp-up on either modern C++ or learning Rust from scratch
